evidence: TASK-027 (R1 no-framework-terms render rule into the shared preamble) via the canonical pipeline. IMPL ef89999: added the plan-register bullet + mirrored FRAG-preamble + re-stamped content_hash + parity 14/14. Adversarial audit returned 3 findings: F1 (preamble over-claimed -- listed bare refraction/weave/front-load as banned, contradicting the harness WARN policy + risking plain-English suppression); F2 (harness HARD list had only American 'behavior-change-primary', British spelling scanned clean); F3 (criterion 1 argued by-construction, not empirically observed). Fix b091792: F1 -> preamble banned-examples aligned to the harness coined-multi-word HARD set, bare singles reframed as plain-OK; F2 -> 'behaviour-change-primary' added to the harness HARD list + test (35 harness tests, 971 planner, gate green); parity 14/14. F3 closed EMPIRICALLY: a fresh opus generation of narrative-draft under the corrected preamble scans check_draft Check 1 (process-method terms) = 0, with all 5 narrative fields substantive + grounded. Orchestrator independently confirmed F1 (preamble grep), F2 (British test), F3 (the fresh-gen scan). Discovered + filed FGAP-032 (all-caps SLO-group-name strand-prefix leak -- a separate register concern, harness Check 2, NOT R1). Scope: prompt-workshop/shared/preamble.md + .workshopping FRAG-preamble (+ check_draft.py/test for F2); no .context/parser/migration.

criteria_results.criterion: A freshly generated plan, scanned against the enumerated process-method-term list, yields zero occurrences.

Compared against the pre-rule draft, the same plan retains its substantive content (all sections present and non-empty; nothing shortened or hollowed to dodge the terms) -- an adversarial reviewer confirms zero substance loss.
Plain descriptive English is still present where apt and is not flagged by the scan (e.g. 'talking at students' passes).
The dispatched output parses clean with no new fabrication flags, and the snippet-substrate parity check passes.
